sections.heading: (1) This Act may be cited as the Zambia Revenue Authority
sections.text:
(1) This Act may be cited as the Zambia Revenue Authority
Short title
(Amendment) Act, 2025, and shall be read as one with the Zambia and commen-
Revenue Authority Act, in this Act referred to as the principal Act. cement
Cap. 321
(2) This Act shall come into operation on 1st January, 2026.

sections.heading: Section 2 of the principal Act is amended by the insertion Amendment
sections.text:
Section 2 of the principal Act is amended by the insertion Amendment
of the following new definitions in the appropriate places in of section 2
alphabetical order:
“Divisions” means the Divisions of the Authority established
by the Commissioner-General in accordance with section
19 (2) of the Act; and
“legal entity” means a company or organisation that has the
capacity to operate, own assets, enter into contracts, sue
and be sued in the company’s or organisation’s own name;.

sections.heading: Section 11(1) of the principal Act is amended by the— Amendment
sections.text:
Section 11(1) of the principal Act is amended by the— Amendment
of section 11
(a) insertion of the following new paragraph immediately after
paragraph (b):
(c) in consultation with the Minister, establish legal
entities to serve as platforms for advancing
innovations, managing large scale projects or
pursuing public private partnerships for the
furtherance of the functions of the Governing
Board; and
Single copies of this Act may be obtained from the Government Printer,
P.O. Box 30136, 10101, Lusaka, Price K4.00 each
308 No. 20 of 2025]
Zambia Revenue Authority (Amendment)
(b) renumbering of paragraph (c) as paragraph (d).
Amendment 4. Section 15 of the principal Act is amended by the deletion
of section 15 of subsection (2) and the substitution therefor of the following:
(2) The Governing Board shall meet for the transaction
of business at least once every three months at a place and
time that the Chairperson may determine.
Amendment 5. Section 21 of the principal Act is amended by the deletion
of section 21 of subsection (4), and the substitution therefor of the following:
Cap. 322 (4) Despite the provisions of the Customs and Excise
Cap.323 Act and the Income Tax Act, nothing in this section shall
prevent the exchange of information between—
(a) the Divisions or Departments in the Authority;
(b) the Divisions or Departments in the Authority and
the Governing Board;
(c) the Divisions or Departments in the Authority and a
legal entity; or
(d) the Authority and a legal entity.
Amendment 6. Section 25 of the principal Act is amended in subsection
of section 25 (1), by the deletion of the word “six” and the substitution therefor
of the word “four”.
